mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0% over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in GDPC1 concurrent with stable CPIAUCSL values indicates real demand expansion rather than nominal price drift. This combination compresses real discount rates and boosts equity risk premiums, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample backtesting, with a cumulative information coefficient below 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial capacity utilization above trend, manufacturing output growth directly translates into higher corporate margins and short-term equity momentum. Rising INDPRO values predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as supply-side shocks feed through to earnings.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical difference from zero when INDPRO rises consecutively for three months, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent acts as a leading indicator of weakening aggregate demand and credit contraction. This deterioration triggers negative earnings revisions and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered episodes, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 per month and a declining unemployment rate confirm structural labor market healing. This dual improvement sustains consumer demand and reduces recession probabilities, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over any 4-year rolling window, indicating the proxies do not jointly predict equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising revolving credit balances indicate expanding consumer leverage and near-term spending momentum. This continuous increase in RSAFS directly fuels short-term equity demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are uncorrelated with RSAFS changes, with a rolling Spearman correlation consistently below 0.10 over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real interest rates turn deeply negative, eroding corporate profit margins and compressing equity valuations. This stagflationary configuration predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during periods of rising CPI and high FEDFUNDS, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below -0.2.
